design_philosophy.core_loop: Place buildings on an isometric map, manage interconnected supply chains (water, food, religion, entertainment, education, health), optimize walker pathfinding to meet citizen needs, balance four rating metrics (Culture, Prosperity, Peace, Favor), and progress through branching mission assignments
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource allocation and infrastructure optimization under time pressure with optional military threats; balancing citizen needs against available space and economic sustainability
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a Roman provincial governor building and managing thriving historical cities across the empire, making tactical choices between peaceful development and military conquest
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The 'walker' mechanic adds strategic depth and randomness—buildings don't automatically serve nearby citizens; service coverage depends on NPC movement paths through roads, forcing deliberate infrastructure design. Rejects SimCity-style static zone influence in favor of dynamic, observable service distribution. Integrates combat into the city-building map rather than separating it, emphasizing unified strategic space. Branching missions (peaceful vs military) force players to choose their challenge type, rejecting one-size-fits-all progression.

difficulty.continues_system: Branching mission structure allows player choice of difficulty path; peaceful assignments have stricter victory conditions but no invasion risk; military assignments include invasion threats but potentially easier resource requirements
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based progression with discrete assignments of increasing complexity; City Construction Kit mode offers sandbox without victory conditions for low-pressure experimentation
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High—players choose peaceful or military variant for each mission; can restart failed assignments without permanent consequences; open-ended City Construction Kit available for skill development

progression.upgrade_system: Implicit through building variety and city size growth; citizens demand increasingly sophisticated services (housing quality improves with access to education, health, entertainment); prosperity and cultural advancement unlock access to better housing tiers
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission completion gates advancement; branching mission paths allow different progression sequences; cultural and prosperity ratings gate access to higher-tier buildings and services
replayability_hooks: Map editor included—players can create custom scenarios from 20+ historical Roman city locations
Branching mission choice (peaceful vs military) creates different playthrough experiences
City Construction Kit sandbox mode for open-ended play without victory conditions
Multiple viable city layouts and supply chain configurations for same objective

world_building_techniques: Historical Roman city settings (Brundisium, Londinium, etc.) establish period atmosphere
Mission briefings frame objectives within Roman imperial context
Caesar requests (tribute, gifts) create narrative motivation for player actions
Disaster events (earthquakes, fires, invasions) as plot developments within city narrative
Advisor dialogue provides contextual guidance and flavor

faction_indicators: Caesar's favor rating—alignment with imperial requests
Enemy faction identity in military missions—determines invader type and threat pattern
Peace rating—indicates citizen satisfaction and absence of unrest
danger_signals: Declining Peace rating signals crime, riots, or invasion risk
Low Favor rating with Caesar indicates potential punishment or mission failure
Disaster warnings (earthquakes, fires) require active mitigation
Military assignment choice explicitly selects for invasion threat
environmental_storytelling: Terrain variation (river channels, fertile land, mining areas) determines local economic opportunities
Historical city layouts reflect actual Roman geography and resource constraints
Building density and placement patterns reveal player's economic strategy and city specialization
Walker movement paths visible on-screen show service distribution effectiveness
